violations.text: The chlorine sanitizer concentration for the sanitizing cycle of the kitchen dishwasher was 0 parts per million (ppm). Chlorine sanitizing solutions must be between 50-100 ppm. Service was called; 3-bay sink was set-up. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/05/2026

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Inspector observed foods placed in plastic containers greater than 4 inches deep. PIC placed foods in shallow pans in ice baths and/or placed ice wands in product. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/19/2026

A container of bolognaise was observed stored on the floor in the basement prep area. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. PIC placed on counter. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/16/2026
The hand-washing sink in the bar was lacking paper towels. Staff provided. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/19/2026

The chlorine sanitizer concentration for the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher was 0 parts per million (ppm). Chlorine sanitizing solutions must be between 50-100 ppm. PIC changed bottle of sanitizer. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/05/2026

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Per observation, sauce was placed hot in the walk-in in covered plastic containers greater than four inches deep. PIC had staff divide into smaller containers. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/19/2026
Refrigerator door gaskets were observed in poor repair. Gaskets have been ordered, awaiting delivery. New Violation. Correct By: 03/19/2026
A hole was observed in the wall at the bottom of the stairwell. New Violation. Correct By: 04/30/2026

Staff were observed pouring liquid into the kitchen hand-washing sink. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. PIC informed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/19/2026
The hand-washing sink in the bar was lacking paper towels. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/19/2026

Molluscan Shellfish (muscles) located in the walk-in did not have the proper identification tags. Molluscan Shellstock tags must remain attached to the container in which the Molluscan Shellstock are received until the container is empty. New Violation. Correct By: 02/19/2026

The chlorine sanitizer concentration for the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher was 0 parts per million (ppm). Chlorine sanitizing solutions must be between 50-100 ppm. PIC called for service; 3-bay sink was set-up during inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 02/19/2026

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (ice machines) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Ice machines taken out of service to be cleaned and sanitized; ice disposed. New Violation. Correct By: 02/19/2026
Working containers used for storing chemicals taken from bulk supplies were not marked with the common name of the chemical. New Violation. Correct By: 03/05/2026

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Inspector observed mashed sweet potatoes in plastic containers greater than 4 inches deep. PIC instructed staff to use shallow containers. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/19/2026

Working containers of dry and liquid ingredients are not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. Staff labeled during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/19/2026
Beverage tubing was observed in direct contact with ice. Staff placed barrier during inspection, ice was disposed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/19/2026

Bowls are being used as scoops in containers of rice, flour, and sauce. Utensils used to dispense non-time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods such as sugar and flour must be stored in the food item with their handles above the top of the food within the container. Bowls were removed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/19/2026
The following were observed in poor repair: -refrigerator door gaskets (reach-in and prep unit) -prep unit (work order in place). New Violation. Correct By: 03/19/2026
The facility is lacking thermo labels or similar internal temperature indicating device for the dish machine at the bar and server station. New Violation. Correct By: 03/05/2026
The non-food contact surfaces of the following have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris: -table under grill -between prep units. New Violation. Correct By: 03/05/2026
A leak was observed at basin drain of the 3-bay sink. New Violation. Correct By: 03/05/2026
The following were observed in poor repair:-corner wall tiles in kitchen-missing ceiling tile near dish wash area-hole in wall (bottom of stairway). New Violation. Correct By: 04/30/2026

The chlorine sanitizer concentration for the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher was 0parts per million (ppm). Chlorine sanitizing solutions must be between 50-100 ppm. All kitchen dishes must be sanitized in 3 bay sink until repaired. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/11/2024

Various foods in the table top prep unit observed held at 50-58 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Food was voluntarily disposed of. Prep unit was observed off. Unit was turned on during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/11/2024
Prep unit on the line is in poor repair. Repairs pending. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/25/2024
Refrigerator door gaskets were observed in poor repair. Per PIC, parts are on order. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/28/2024

Raw animal foods (such as meatballs) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (such as vegetables and garnishes) in the walk-in 2 door lowboy. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/28/2024

The chlorine sanitizer concentration for the sanitizing cycle of the kitchen dishwasher was parts per million 0 (ppm). Chlorine sanitizing solutions must be between 50-100 ppm. Service called during time of inspection. All dishes to be sanitized in 3 bay sink. New Violation. Correct By: 03/28/2024

Hollandaise was observed held at (113) degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Food was not reheated due to quality issues. Food was voluntarily disposed of. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/28/2024

Various Foods were observed held at (43-53) degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. TCS foods were voluntarily disposed of during time of inspection. Foods prepared today were placed over ice and/or into other refrigeration. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/28/2024
Housemade mushroom broth was observed held past 7 days. Food was voluntarily disposed of. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/28/2024

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Per interview of PIC, mushroom broth was placed hot in the refrigerator in containers greater than four inches deep. Food was placed into smaller containers to continue cooling process. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/28/2024

ROP Lobster was observed thawing with packaging still intact at 38 degrees Fahrenheit. Reduced Oxygen Packaged seafood must be opened prior to thawing. Package was opened during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/28/2024
Ice was observed in direct contact with cold plates and soda lines. PIC informed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/28/2024

Prep Unit on the line was observed at an ambient of 46 degrees Fahrenheit. Unit was taken out of service during time of inspection. Equipment cannot be used until properly adjusted/ repaired/ replaced. New Violation. Correct By: 04/11/2024
Refrigerator door gaskets were observed in poor repair. New Violation. Correct By: 04/28/2024
Pipe under 3 bay sink was observed leaking water into a bucket. Plumbing systems to be in good repair and proper adjustment. New Violation. Correct By: 04/11/2024
The doors to all toilet rooms are not self-closing. New Violation. Correct By: 04/11/2024

Shellfish tags must be maintained for 90 days in chronological order and the date when the last shellfish was used from the bag must be noted on the shellfish tag. New Violation. Correct By: 07/18/2023

The presence of small flies is not controlled as evidenced by live insects observed during inspection. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 07/18/2023
Soda syrups and oil were observed stored on the floor in the basement. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 07/18/2023

High temperature glass washer at bar was not reaching proper temperature for sanitization. Equipment was taken out of service during inspection. Equipment must be in good repair prior to use. New Violation. Correct By: 07/25/2023
The non-food contact surfaces of the prep unit and lowboy refrigeration units have an accumulation of black residue, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 07/25/2023
Coving by kitchen entrance is in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 08/18/2023
